title: Regional grad program is expanding
full_text:
Westpac is expanding its regional graduate program to two additional locations in its second year, building on the success of its inaugural 2026 cohort and reinforcing its investment in regional Australia.
Part of Westpac's broader graduate offering, the 2027 program will provide regional placements across 12 centres, giving university graduates the opportunity to launch their careers without leaving their local communities. The number of regional placements will also grow from 10 to 15 graduates across the country.
"The whole idea is that we take on graduates who come from regional Australia, and we place them in regional Australia," Peta Ward, managing director, regional commercial and agribusiness said.
"We want young people who have that real connection to their community, and found this was a really good opportunity for us to give opportunities to young people who wanted to stay in regional Australia."
Designed in partnership with Westpac's Regional and Agribusiness team, the program combines hands-on experience with structured learning.
"I always say to young people when they join banking, it's a life skill. What you will learn from working in a bank, these skills are transferable wherever you end up in your career or where life takes you... banking is a great foundation," Ms Ward said.
"I think if you're passionate about regional Australia, and you see yourself having a great career in regional Australia, and you're connected to agribusiness or regional commercial, then absolutely put your application in."
Roddy Packer is one of 10 students taking part in the program this year, drawn to banking by the opportunity to directly impact customers' livelihoods. Raised on a cattle and broadacre property near Roma, Mr Packer applied for the graduate program after studying agribusiness at The University of Queensland.
He said the best part about the program was being able to create lasting connections, with not only the people he works alongside at Westpac, but the customers he has met throughout the year.
"My lightbulb moment was realising that banking isn't just numbers. There's relationships and connections to be built behind those numbers, and in reality, that's the most important thing, being able to connect with customers," Mr Packer said.
"It's definitely not a path I thought I would originally go down, but I'm very glad I have. Banking is such a great foundation for any career, and life in general, and the support network and people you meet within Westpac has been invaluable."
Graduates complete four rotations across lending, credit risk, portfolio management, strategy and ESG, while working directly with local businesses and customers. Graduates who complete the program are well placed to move into ongoing roles within Westpac's Regional and Agribusiness business, continuing to grow their careers in the communities they support and are based in.
Regional placement locations span Rockhampton, Toowoomba, Townsville, Naracoorte, Tamworth, Wagga Wagga, Orange, Bendigo, Ballarat and Launceston, with additional regional locations to join the line-up in 2027, to be confirmed later this year. Placements are also available for regional graduates who want to be based in Perth, Brisbane, Melbourne or Adelaide.
The program is a key part of Westpac's broader investment in regional Australia, helping strengthen local industries and support long-term economic growth. By creating career pathways locally, it supports talent retention and builds capability where it's needed most.
The Federal Capital Press of Australia Pty Ltd
